$comment: Source of truth for filename conventions of AI-generated documents. Read by .claude/hooks/enforce-generated-doc-names.js (PreToolUse enforcement) and .claude/scripts/validate-generated-doc-names.js (repo-wide audit). Human-readable mirror: .claude/shared/naming-conventions.md

matching.description: Each convention is considered in scope for a file when its dirGlob matches the file's parent directory. A file is OK for a convention when its basename matches filenamePattern. A file is DRIFT for a convention when its basename matches badPattern but NOT filenamePattern. If no convention classes the file as OK or DRIFT, it is ungoverned and ignored.

conventions.rationale: Epic number lives in the parent directory name. Putting it in the filename too makes /story-(\d+)/ parsers capture the epic number instead of the story number — breaking workflow-helpers.js lines 147, 441, 923 and the dashboard's Test Coverage card.
Leading underscore keeps the overview sorted above story files in directory listings. Exact filename; no per-epic suffix.
feature-planner.md:195 calls the exact path CRITICAL — getPhases(), dashboard, and traceability all read this exact filename and silently fall back to 'no phasing' if it's elsewhere.
Leading underscore keeps it sorted above the epic directories. Both .md (human) and .json (machine) flavours exist.
Same pattern as story files — no epic prefix. The spec-compliance-watchdog and test-generator discover this file via /story-M-/ glob expansion; an extra epic number in the filename breaks discovery.
code-reviewer.md:205 and :483 glob for story-M-*-test-handoff.md. Extra epic number breaks those globs.
orchestrator-rules.md:628 and continue.md:534 read this file verbatim as the single source of truth for re-verification prompts during QA fix cycles.
Review markers live in a flat directory (no epic subfolders), so BOTH epic and story numbers must be in the filename. validate-phase-output.js:626 enforces this exact shape.
Flat directory, epic-level marker. validate-phase-output.js:635 enforces this exact shape.
E2E specs live in a flat directory. orchestrator-rules.md:518 globs them as epic-<N>-story-<M>-*.spec.ts — both numbers are required for the E2E Verification step to resolve the combined target set.
